title: Acute sprain
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Supports pain, swelling, bruising, restricted movement, weight-bearing difficulty, instability, loss of function, mechanism, timing, force, direction, pop or snap, aggravating conditions, and previous episodes.
presenting_history.rationale: Directly supports mapped sprain and strain terminology, mechanisms, symptoms, function, recurrence, and assessed findings.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports pain, swelling, bruising, restricted movement, weight-bearing difficulty, instability, loss of function, mechanism, timing, force, direction, pop or snap, aggravating conditions, and previous episodes.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Muscle Sprains & Strains – 47

red_flags.text: Identifies severe pain or swelling, discoloration, weakness, movement loss, numbness, coldness, deformity, persistent symptoms, fracture or dislocation concern, and clinician-selected radiography, ultrasound, or MRI.
red_flags.rationale: Directly supports mapped deformity, movement, neurovascular, severe-swelling, pop, and selected X-ray concern fields.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Identifies severe pain or swelling, discoloration, weakness, movement loss, numbness, coldness, deformity, persistent symptoms, fracture or dislocation concern, and clinician-selected radiography, ultrasound, or MRI.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Muscle Sprains & Strains – 47

red_flags.text: Supports clinician-documented RICE, activity protection, mobilization, analgesia, physical therapy, return to activity, one-week review, worsening review, and urgent escalation for severe, neurovascular, deformity, weight-bearing, fever, or hot-joint concerns.
red_flags.rationale: Directly supports mapped clinician-documented RICE, activity, analgesia, return, review, and follow-up fields.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-documented RICE, activity protection, mobilization, analgesia, physical therapy, return to activity, one-week review, worsening review, and urgent escalation for severe, neurovascular, deformity, weight-bearing, fever, or hot-joint concerns.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Muscle Sprains & Strains – 47

follow_up.text: Defines sprains and strains, common ankle, knee, wrist and muscle contexts, Dubai telehealth scope, injury mechanisms, athletic activity, overexertion, repetitive motion, and recurrence risks.
follow_up.rationale: Reviewed to establish sprain and strain scope; no additional broad injury items were mapped from scope alone.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Defines sprains and strains, common ankle, knee, wrist and muscle contexts, Dubai telehealth scope, injury mechanisms, athletic activity, overexertion, repetitive motion, and recurrence risks.

source_limitations: Open-wound, formal ligament-stress-test, exact fixed 48-hour weight-bearing, and broad joint-specific examination fields remain unsupported.
The ligament-injury label must remain clinician-stated and grade dependent.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
